categories.name: Surface-definition answers
categories.description: The model answered the clue as a normal definition or surface description, rather than looking for the cryptic or joking answer intended by the puzzle.

categories.name: Compound/charade pun not built
categories.description: The correct answer is a compound or constructed phrase combining two readings of clue elements; the model failed to decompose and recombine the parts cryptically.

categories.name: Foreign, regional, abbreviation cue missed
categories.description: Markers such as Eng., Fr., Gr., Vl., place names, acronyms, or Belgian/Dutch cultural references were ignored or handled in the wrong language/register.

categories.name: Letter/sound-level manipulation missed
categories.description: The solution depends on hidden letters, sound-alikes, spelling changes, resegmentation, or other word-level mechanics; the model stayed at the semantic level.
example_clues: O mengelmoes, o potpourri. O velerlei, ...

categories.name: Malformed or copied clue fragments
categories.description: The model produced a non-answer by copying, concatenating, or slightly altering words from the clue, sometimes yielding a non-word or tautological answer.

categories.name: Wrong sense, register, or domain of an ambiguous word
categories.description: The model chose a plausible sense of the clue word, but the intended answer relies on a less obvious meaning, register, idiom, or specialist domain.
